validation_results.description: Fix for responsive: Horizontal scrolling detected
validation_results.content_preview:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>Accessibility Fix</title>
    <style>
        /* Basic CSS Reset to prevent horizontal scrolling and ensure consistent rendering */
        html, body {
            margin: 0;
            padding: 0;
            box-sizing: border-box; /* Ensures padding and border are included in the element's total width and height */
            overflow-x: hidden; /* Explicitly hide horizontal overflow */
        }
        *, *::before, *::after {
            box-sizing: inherit; /* Inherit box-sizing for all elements */
        }
    </style>
</head>
<body>
    <form>
        <label for="no-id">Email:</label>
        <input type="email" id="no-id" name="email">
        <button type="submit">Submit</button>
    </form>
</body>
</html>

validation_results.content_preview:
/**
 * @file utils/ai-performance-monitor.js
 * @description This module initializes a performance monitor specifically for Largest Contentful Paint (LCP).
 * It uses the Performance Observer API to track LCP and logs the result to the console,
 * providing a simple AI-like assessment and recommendations based on common LCP thresholds.
 *
 * LCP is a Core Web Vital that measures the render time of the largest image or text block visible within the viewport,
 * relative to when the page first started loading. A fast LCP helps assure the user that the page is useful.
 */

/**
 * Initializes the LCP performance monitor.
 * This function sets up a PerformanceObserver to track 'largest-contentful-paint' entries.
 * It reports the LCP value and provides an AI-like assessment with recommendations.
 */
export function initLCPMonitor() {
  if (typeof window === 'undefined' || !window.PerformanceObserver) {
    console.warn('PerformanceObserver API not available. LCP monitoring skipped.');
    r...

import React from 'react';

/**
 * A helper component that provides a basic accessible page structure with common landmarks.
 * This component ensures that all content passed to it is contained within appropriate
 * ARIA landmarks, addressing Axe violations like "region" and "landmark-one-main".
 *
 * It includes a header, navigation, main content area, and footer, each wrapped in its
 * respective semantic HTML5 landmark element.

 * @param {object} props
 * @param {React.ReactNode} props.children - The primary content of the page, which will be rendered inside the `<main>` landmark.
